title: Forge recce plugin: fix ch3-join-shift false positives (plugin 9/12 → 11/12+)
description:
Plugin's lineage tools cause agent to over-report impact on ch3-join-shift (clean-profile mode).
With-plugin scores 9/12 vs baseline 11/12 — 3 false positives: customers, customer_segments, customer_order_pattern.
These models read stg_payments/stg_orders directly, not through orders, so join key change in orders.sql doesn't affect them.

Root cause: plugin lineage tools give a flat 'all downstream' list without distinguishing direct vs alternative-path dependencies.

Target: with-plugin >= 11/12 on ch3-join-shift (clean-profile).
Eval command: /recce-eval run --case ch3-join-shift -n 1 (with --clean-profile)

title: Run full clean-profile -n 3 suite for all ch3 scenarios
description:
Need -n 3 runs for all ch3 scenarios in clean-profile mode to measure variance:
- ch3-phantom-filter: baseline 10/12, plugin 12/12 (n=1 only)
- ch3-join-shift: baseline 11/12, plugin 9/12 (n=1 only)
- ch3-count-distinct: baseline 11/12, plugin 11/12 (n=1 only)

Single-run results may be noisy. -n 3 needed to confirm patterns are stable.
Should be done AFTER forge improvements to ch3-join-shift false positives.

Estimated cost: ~$12, ~30 min

title: recce-docs MCP server implementation
close_reason: Implemented recce-docs MCP server with 4 tools: searchDocs, getPage, listSections, syncDocs. Integrated into recce-quickstart plugin.

title: /recce-ci command implementation
description: Implement CI/CD setup command based on design doc. Supports standalone workflow, existing CI integration, monorepo, auto-detect adapter.
close_reason: Completed /recce-ci command implementation with all 6 steps: environment detection, CI check, Recce Cloud project reminder, standalone/integration workflow generation, secrets guide, and commit handling

title: Eval scoring: affected_row_count tolerance instead of exact match
description:
All ch3 scenarios fail affected_row_count for BOTH variants in clean-profile mode:
- ch3-phantom-filter: 2326 → 15 (agent counted affected customers, not payments)
- ch3-join-shift: 100463 → 97221 (agent counted matched rows, not total)
- ch3-count-distinct: 334 → 253 (agent counted changed days, not total)

Options:
1. Tolerance-based scoring (±20% or order-of-magnitude match)
2. Remove affected_row_count from deterministic checks, move to judge criteria
3. Keep exact match but provide clearer prompt guidance

This is a scoring methodology issue, not a plugin issue.

title: Self-forge: recce-eval should use --clean-profile as default for forge TDD
description:
Current forge workflow (kc-plugin-forge) runs eval without --clean-profile, meaning TDD results are inflated by accumulated memory. Forge should simulate real new-user conditions.

Action items:
1. Update recce-dev forge config or SKILL.md to recommend --clean-profile for TDD runs
2. Consider making --clean-profile the default in run-case.sh (opt-out with --with-memory)
3. Document the methodological reasoning: forge results must be reproducible by other users

Context: ch3-phantom-filter baseline drops from 12/12 to 10/12 without memory — memory inflation is real and significant.
